summary: Live npm publish token for @arbocollab scope shipped in tarball
details: The published tarball contains `npmjs.npmrc` with an active npm auth token scoped to the `@arbocollab` registry namespace (`//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=npm_<redacted>` alongside `@arbocollab:registry=https://registry.npmjs.org`). Any party who installs or downloads this package obtains a working publish credential for the `@arbocollab` scope and can push malicious versions of any `@arbocollab/*` package, attacking every downstream consumer of that scope. The leakage is enabled by `package.json` declaring `"files": ["*"]` together with a publish flow that uses `npm publish --userconfig=npmjs.npmrc`, which causes the credential file used to authenticate the publish to be included in the published artifact. This is distribution of a third-party credential that gives attackers a supply-chain pivot, not author-self-harm — the harm to downstream installers comes from the published-version channel the leaked token controls.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
